engineIntelligence.h2: Chrysler Pacifica Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Chrysler Pacifica in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Chrysler Pacifica ERB engine - petrol 3.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain wear - causes rattling noise and potential cam phaser failure around 80k-100k miles.
Excessive oil consumption - linked to piston ring wear on higher-mileage units.

cards.altText: Chrysler Pacifica VM64C engine - diesel 2.8L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injector failure - leads to rough idling, diesel knock, and excessive smoke.
Turbocharger degradation - causes significant loss of power and whining noises.

cards.altText: Chrysler Pacifica ENS engine - diesel 2.8L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR valve clogging - results in loss of power, limp mode, and failed MOTs.
Oil sludge buildup - due to missed service intervals, causing premature bearing wear.

engineGuide.h2: Chrysler Pacifica Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Chrysler Pacifica across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: The most common 2.8L CRD code for the UK-market Grand Voyager, offering strong torque but prone to injector and turbo issues.
compatibleVariants: Grand Voyager RT, Grand Voyager Limited
commonFailures: Injector failure causing rough idling and diesel knock (60k-90k miles).
Turbocharger degradation leading to power loss.
entries.cta: Get quotes for VM64C Chrysler Pacifica engine replacement

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: An earlier variant of the 2.8L CRD, sharing architecture with the VM64C but with different ECU mappings for base SE models.
compatibleVariants: Grand Voyager SE, Grand Voyager RT
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging causing limp mode and failed MOTs.
Oil sludge buildup from missed service intervals.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ENS Chrysler Pacifica engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: The flagship Pentastar V6 powering the modern Pacifica, delivering smooth performance but requiring vigilance against timing chain wear.
commonFailures: Timing chain wear and cam phaser rattle (80k-100k miles).
Excessive oil consumption due to piston ring wear.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ERB Chrysler Pacifica engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: Functionally identical to the ERB, the ERC code is often found in specific trim levels or later production runs of the 3.6L V6.
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch leading to check engine lights.
Head gasket failure if overheating occurs.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ERC Chrysler Pacifica engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: Another iteration of the 3.6L Pentastar, primarily used in top-tier trims requiring strict emissions compliance.
commonFailures: Cylinder misfires (P0302) due to internal valvetrain wear.
Oil cooler leaks mixing coolant and oil.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ERF Chrysler Pacifica engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Chrysler Pacifica Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Chrysler Pacifica is a robust family MPV, but specific engine families dominate replacement demand, notably the 3.6L Pentastar V6 (ERB/ERC) and the 2.8L CRD (VM64C/ENS). Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this guide to decide your best path forward.

problems.h4: Chrysler 3.6L Pentastar Timing Chain Failure - Why Pacifica Engines Rattle
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Pacifica Touring, Limited, Pinnacle (2017-2024, ERB/ERC/ERF)
problems.rootCause: The timing chain and plastic guides wear prematurely, stretching the chain and causing cam phaser rattle and potential catastrophic failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, new chain, guides, tensioners, and cam phasers.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before valve/piston contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a fully rebuilt 3.6L engine with upgraded chain components and 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution once catastrophic rattling begins.

problems.recommendation: If you hear a distinct ticking or rattling noise on cold starts, do not delay. A preventative chain kit is cost-effective, but once the chain has jumped, a full rebuilt engine replacement is the only reliable option to protect your investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt ERB engine prices for your Chrysler Pacifica

problems.h4: Chrysler 2.8L CRD Injector & Turbo Failure - Why Voyager Engines Knock
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Grand Voyager RT, Limited (2008-2015, VM64C/ENS)
problems.rootCause: Diesel injector seal failure allows combustion gases to escape, while turbocharger wear leads to metal shavings contaminating the intake.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and replace all injectors, turbocharger, and clean the intake manifold.
repairOptions.longevity: Only recommended if the engine block and bearings are confirmed healthy.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a remanufactured 2.8L CRD engine with new injectors, turbo, and warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: The definitive fix for advanced knock or metallic debris in the sump.

problems.recommendation: A failing turbo often contaminates the entire engine with metal shavings. We strongly recommend a complete reconditioned 2.8L CRD engine to guarantee no residual damage remains, providing peace of mind for heavy family use.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt VM64C engines for your Chrysler Pacifica

problems.h4: Chrysler Pacifica Excessive Oil Consumption - Pentastar Piston Ring Wear
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Pacifica Touring, Touring L (2017-2024, ERB)
problems.rootCause: Factory piston rings allow oil to slip into the combustion chamber, leading to blue exhaust smoke, fouled spark plugs, and low oil warnings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace piston rings, valve stem seals, and clean carbon buildup.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if the engine block is not scored and bearings are intact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a rebuilt 3.6L engine with updated piston rings and 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: The safest route if the engine has suffered from prolonged oil starvation.

problems.recommendation: Regular oil level checks are critical. If your Pacifica is burning more than a litre per 1,000 miles, a full rebuilt engine with updated components is more economical than repeated top-end repairs.
problems.cta: Compare 3.6L Pentastar ERB engine prices for your Chrysler Pacifica

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Chrysler Pacifica
finalCta.paragraph: Navigating engine problems on a Chrysler Pacifica can be stressful, but EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists. We connect you directly with vetted rebuilders who supply fully tested, upgraded engines backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, with nationwide delivery and supply & fit options available. Avoid the main dealer premium and get back on the road with confidence.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Chrysler Pacifica Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Chrysler Pacifica and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Chrysler Pacifica Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel variants, particularly the 2.8L CRD, dominated the UK-market Grand Voyager lineup, offering strong torque and motorway efficiency, but requiring specialist rebuilds to address injector and turbo failures.
items.descriptor: Diesel variants, particularly the 2.8L CRD, dominated the UK-market Grand Voyager lineup, offering strong torque and motorway efficiency, but requiring specialist rebuilds to address injector and turbo failures.

ENS - 2.8L CRD, early model unit prone to EGR clogging and oil sludge.
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and high torque for towing.
EGR and DPF issues on vehicles primarily used for short urban journeys.
High UK replacement demand on Grand Voyager RT and Limited trims.

families: Hybrid V6 - Plug-in Hybrid powertrain with dedicated high-voltage integration.
48-volt starter-generator recovers braking energy for the eTorque system.
Electric torque assistance under acceleration improves fuel economy.
Battery health check is highly recommended during any engine replacement.

engineYears.h2: Chrysler Pacifica Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: Use this Chrysler Pacifica year guide to match your vehicle to the right engine family, production period, and replacement options.

years.description: The original Chrysler Pacifica arrived, offering a unique crossover MPV experience with a range of V6 petrol options.
keyChanges: Launch of the first-generation Chrysler Pacifica crossover.
Introduction of the 2.7L (EER), 3.3L (EGC), and 3.5L (EGJ) V6 petrol engines.
Early models featured basic trim levels focused on family practicality.
knownFor: Unique crossover styling that blended MPV space with SUV looks.
3.5L EGJ engines prone to head gasket issues if cooling systems were neglected.
Strong residual values for well-maintained, low-mileage examples today.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004-2008 Chrysler Pacifica replacement engines
years.ticker: Chrysler Pacifica 3.5 V6 head gasket replacement • Chrysler Pacifica 2.7 V6 oil sludge fix
enquiries: Chrysler Pacifica 3.5 V6 head gasket replacement
Chrysler Pacifica 2.7 V6 oil sludge fix

years.description: Chrysler rebranded the Pacifica as the Grand Voyager in the UK, introducing robust diesel options to cater to the European market's demand for torque and efficiency.
keyChanges: Rebranding to Chrysler Grand Voyager for the UK market.
Introduction of the 2.8L CRD diesel (ENS, VM64C) and 3.8L V6 petrol (EGH).
Phasing out of the smaller 2.7L and 3.3L petrol units.
knownFor: The 2.8L CRD becoming the definitive, most-searched engine for replacement.
Injector and turbocharger failures emerging as the primary failure points for the VM64C engine.
Strong towing capability making the RT and Limited trims popular with caravan owners.
years.ticker: Chrysler Grand Voyager 2.8 CRD injector replacement • Chrysler Voyager turbo failure fix
enquiries: Chrysler Grand Voyager 2.8 CRD injector replacement

years.description: The Pacifica name returned, bringing a modern, efficient 3.6L Pentastar V6, advanced safety features, and the introduction of Hybrid and eTorque powertrains.
keyChanges: Return of the "Pacifica" nameplate in the UK via imports and specialist channels.
Introduction of the 3.6L Pentastar V6 (ERB, ERC, ERF) as the standard engine.
Launch of the Plug-in Hybrid and eTorque mild-hybrid variants.
knownFor: The 3.6L Pentastar offering much better refinement and fuel economy than its predecessors.
New failure patterns emerging, specifically timing chain stretch and excessive oil consumption.
Highly complex electronics making DIY engine swaps impossible, driving "supply & fit" demand.
years.cta: Find a rebuilt 2017-2024 Chrysler Pacifica engine today

faq.h2: Chrysler Pacifica Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Chrysler Pacifica engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A used Chrysler Pacifica engine typically costs between £1,200 and £2,000, while a fully reconditioned unit ranges from £2,500 to £4,000, excluding VAT and fitting labour.
keyPoints: 3.6L Pentastar (ERB) rebuilt units average £1,800 to £3,200.
2.8L CRD (VM64C) rebuilt units average £1,600 to £2,800.
Supply and fit service includes labour, fluids, and a unified warranty.
Prices exclude ancillary parts like new DPFs or EGR valves if required.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Chrysler Pacifica engine prices today

items.question: What is the most common engine problem in the Chrysler Pacifica?
items.answer: The most frequent catastrophic failures are timing chain wear in the 3.6L Pentastar V6 (ERB) and injector/turbo failures in the 2.8L CRD (VM64C). Both issues are well-documented and directly addressed in our rebuilt units.
keyPoints: ERB timing chains often fail between 80,000 and 100,000 miles.
VM64C injectors degrade, causing diesel knock and rough idling.
Ignoring early rattling or knocking noises guarantees expensive internal damage.
Rebuilt engines feature upgraded chains or valvetrain components to prevent recurrence.
items.cta: Get a quote for a fixed 3.6L Pentastar or 2.8L CRD replacement

items.question: How long does a Chrysler Pacifica engine supply and fit take?
items.answer: The typical turnaround time for a Chrysler Pacifica engine replacement is 3 to 5 working days from the point of order confirmation. This includes sourcing the unit, palletised delivery, and professional garage installation.
keyPoints: Sourcing and dispatching the rebuilt engine takes 1-2 working days.
Professional supply and fit installation requires 1-2 days in the garage.
Complex variants like the Hybrid may require slight additional sourcing time.
We coordinate directly with the garage to minimise your vehicle's downtime.
items.cta: Arrange a fast supply and fit for your Chrysler Pacifica

items.question: What warranty comes with a reconditioned Chrysler Pacifica engine?
items.answer: Every reconditioned or rebuilt engine supplied through our network includes a minimum 12-month, unlimited mileage warranty as standard. This covers both parts and labour, providing complete peace of mind.
keyPoints: The warranty is valid across the entire UK through our approved garage network.
It specifically covers internal mechanical failures of the supplied unit.
Regular servicing with the correct oil specifications must be maintained.
Always confirm the specific warranty documentation with your chosen supplier before fitting.
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Chrysler Pacifica engine with 12-month warranty

items.question: Can I replace my 3.6L Pentastar with a remanufactured unit?
items.answer: Absolutely. A remanufactured 3.6L Pentastar is built to strict factory specifications, often featuring upgraded components that make it more reliable than the original unit, making it the ultimate choice for longevity.
keyPoints: Remanufactured engines are completely stripped, cleaned, and machined.
Addresses all known design flaws of the original Chrysler Pentastar.
Includes comprehensive bench testing and the most robust warranty coverage.
Ideal for owners planning to keep their Pacifica for another 100,000+ miles.
items.cta: Compare remanufactured 3.6L Pentastar engine costs

items.question: How do I know which engine code my Chrysler Pacifica has?
items.answer: You can find your engine code in three places: on your V5C logbook (Section D.2), on the manufacturer's VIN plate under the bonnet, or stamped directly onto the engine block near the cylinder head.
keyPoints: The V5C registration document is the fastest and most reliable method.
Common codes are ERB (3.6 Petrol), VM64C (2.8 Diesel), and ENS (2.8 Diesel).
Providing the exact code guarantees 100% compatibility for your replacement.
Our registration lookup tool can also identify this automatically for you.
items.cta: Use our free instant Chrysler Pacifica engine code checker

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Chrysler Pacifica?
items.answer: Yes, replacing the engine is often significantly more cost-effective than purchasing a new vehicle, especially given the Pacifica's enduring comfort, space, and the high cost of modern depreciation.
keyPoints: A rebuilt engine restores the car to better-than-used reliability.
It costs a fraction of the monthly repayments on a new family MPV.
The vehicle retains its core value, interior luxury, and driving dynamics.
Supply and fit options protect you from unexpected mechanic markups.
items.cta: See if a Chrysler Pacifica engine replacement is worth it for you

items.question: Do you offer nationwide supply and fit for Chrysler Pacifica engines?
items.answer: Yes, we offer a comprehensive, nationwide supply and fit service, connecting you with vetted, specialist garages across the UK who are experienced in handling heavy V6 engine installations.
keyPoints: We handle the logistics, delivering the engine directly to your chosen garage.
All partner garages are reviewed and rated by other Chrysler owners.
You receive a single, unified warranty covering both the engine and the labour.
No need to source a separate mechanic or arrange complex engine transportation.
items.cta: Book a nationwide supply and fit for your Chrysler Pacifica

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned Pacifica engines?
items.answer: While often used interchangeably, "reconditioned" typically implies a more thorough, ground-up restoration to OEM specifications, whereas "rebuilt" may focus on replacing only the specific failed components and associated wear items.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engines are completely stripped, cleaned, and machined.
Rebuilt engines address the root cause of the specific failure (e.g., timing chain).
Both options from our network undergo rigorous bench testing before dispatch.
Both come with the same robust 12-month, unlimited mileage warranty.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned Chrysler Pacifica engine prices

items.question: Does the replacement engine come with an ECU?
items.answer: No, replacement engines are supplied as long or short blocks and do not include the Engine Control Unit (ECU). Your vehicle's existing ECU is retained, reinstalled, and cleared of fault codes by the fitting garage.
keyPoints: The ECU is coded to your vehicle's chassis and immobiliser system.
Swapping the ECU would require complex, expensive dealer-level reprogramming.
Specialist garages will perform an ECU reset to integrate the new engine seamlessly.
Ensure your garage has the diagnostic tools required for the ECU adaptation.
items.cta: Get quotes for Chrysler Pacifica engine supply only
